item.title: Zimbabwe’s Pension Funds Are Sitting on a Property Time Bomb
item.description: IN most countries, pension funds are the ultimate symbols of financial patience, long-term capital steered cautiously to protect the dignity of retirement. In Zimbabwe, however, a growing concentration of pension savings in premium real estate raises uncomfortable questions about whether prudence has quietly given way to speculation dressed up as strategy. By Brighton Musonza At […]The post Zimbabwe’s Pension Funds Are Sitting on a Property Time Bomb appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Re-industrialising Zimbabwe: From Extraction to Production in a Fragmented Global Economy – Comparative Lessons and Strategic Imperatives
item.description: Zimbabwe’s economic predicament is often attributed to currency instability, sanctions, or governance failures. While these factors are real and influential, they obscure a deeper structural issue: Zimbabwe is not impoverished because it lacks resources, talent, or geography. By Brighton Musonza Its challenge is fundamentally structural—an economy that has failed to industrialise. Without a productive industrial […]The post Re-industrialising Zimbabwe: From Extraction to Production in a Fragmented Global Economy – Comparative Lessons and Strategic Imperatives appeared first on The...

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Econet InfraCo and Cassava Technologies: Engineering Africa’s Distributed Digital Infrastructure Economy
item.description: The emergence and listing of Econet InfraCo represents a decisive structural shift in how African telecommunications groups are reconfiguring themselves for the data economy. No longer confined to voice and connectivity, the sector is undergoing a deep transformation into a capital-intensive, compute-driven infrastructure class. When viewed in tandem with Cassava Technologies and its backbone network […]The post Econet InfraCo and Cassava Technologies: Engineering Africa’s Distributed Digital Infrastructure Economy appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: First Mutual Returns to Profit, but Property Gains Mask Underwriting Pressures
item.description: HARARE – First Mutual Holdings has reported a return to profitability for the year ended 31 December 2025, posting a net profit of US$14.3 million after a US$26.3 million loss in the prior year. However, analysts caution that the recovery was largely driven by property revaluation gains rather than core insurance performance. According to Equity […]The post First Mutual Returns to Profit, but Property Gains Mask Underwriting Pressures appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Iran Seizes Two Tankers in Strait of Hormuz, Raising Tensions in Key Global Shipping Route
item.description: TEHRAN/DUBAI – Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has seized two commercial vessels in the strategically important Strait of Hormuz, increasing tensions in one of the world’s most critical oil transit corridors. According to Iranian state-linked media, citing an official statement from the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps Navy, the vessels MSC Francesca and Epaminondas were […]The post Iran Seizes Two Tankers in Strait of Hormuz, Raising Tensions in Key Global Shipping Route appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Telecel Zimbabwe Opens Investor Search as Rescue Plan Gains Momentum
item.description: HARARE – Telecel Zimbabwe entered corporate rescue after a prolonged period of financial strain marked by mounting debt obligations, declining subscriber numbers, and intensifying competition in Zimbabwe’s telecoms sector. The company has struggled to keep pace with larger rivals, particularly in network investment and service quality, which has eroded its market share over time. The […]The post Telecel Zimbabwe Opens Investor Search as Rescue Plan Gains Momentum appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Ariston Bets on New CEO as Turnaround Nears Inflection Point Amid Climate and Funding Pressure
item.description: HARARE – Ariston Holdings Limited has appointed Farai Madziva as its new Chief Executive Officer, marking a leadership transition the board says will steer the agro-industrial group through the final stage of its turnaround and capital-raising programme. According to Equity Axis News, Madziva officially assumed the role following the departure of outgoing CEO Leon Nortier […]The post Ariston Bets on New CEO as Turnaround Nears Inflection Point Amid Climate and Funding Pressure appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Highlanders in Deep Financial Crisis As Players Boycott Training, Mwaruwari Owed Salaries
item.description: [New Zimbabwe] Highlanders Football Club are facing a deepening financial crisis, with players reportedly going unpaid since February.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Government Pleads With Striking Nurses to Return to Work
item.description: [New Zimbabwe] Discover moreZimbabwean art printsCyber security solutionsZimbabwean fashion apparelZimbabwe's government scrambled into crisis mode on Monday as a nationwide nurses' strike crippled public hospitals, forcing Health Minister Douglas Mombeshora to make an urgent appeal for the healthworkers to return to work while promising to address their grievances.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: PSL to Probe Hardrock FC Marshal's Altercation With Supporter
item.description: [New Zimbabwe] The Zimbabwe Premier Soccer League (PSL) has launched an investigation into a violent incident involving a Hardrock FC marshal and a supporter during a league match played over the weekend.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Man Axes Stepmother, Sets Her On Fire
item.description: [New Zimbabwe] Discover moreWorld news updatesDigital marketing servicesZimbabwean sports merchandiseA 20-year-old Lupane man who struck his elderly stepmother with an axe before setting her on fire in a dispute over cooking oil has been jailed for 16 years.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: African Sun exits VFEX, opts for unlisted status
item.description: HARARE,– Hospitality group African Sun Limited has delisted from the Victoria Falls Stock Exchange (VFEX), signalling a strategic shift to operate as an unlisted public company. In a notice to shareholders, the group confirmed it had met all regulatory conditions for the voluntary termination of its listing, with trading in its shares ceasing on April […]The post African Sun exits VFEX, opts for unlisted status appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra review – A productivity and privacy-first flagship smartphone
item.description: Modern smartphones have become mobile workstations – and after spending a few days with the Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra, it’s clear this device is designed with that shift in mind. This is a flagship that prioritises productivity, security, and efficiency, while still delivering top-tier performance and creative capability when needed. It feels particularly suited to […]The post Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra review – A productivity and privacy-first flagship smartphone appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: High Court finds miners in contempt over Lonrho site
item.description: The High Court of Zimbabwe has declared a mining company and its officials in contempt of court for defying an interdict barring operations at the disputed Lonrho Mining site. In Asmdev Incorporated (Pvt) Ltd v Joh and Lewis Investment (Pvt) Ltd and Others (544/26) [2026] ZWHHC 130, Justice Mambara ruled that the respondents wilfully breached […]The post High Court finds miners in contempt over Lonrho site appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: State not ready to prosecute Biti
item.description: The trial of former Finance Minister and Constitution Defenders Forum (CDF) Convener Tendai Biti, who is facing charges of holding a public meeting without notifying the regulating authority, has been postponed to May 26, 2026. The matter was deferred on Monday by Mutare Magistrate Honest Musiiwa after the State indicated it was not ready to […]The post State not ready to prosecute Biti appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

The SRC stated that the suspensions follow a prolonged period of instability within Zimbabwe Aquatics

channel.item: Is ZANU-PF so useless that it can’t continue development without Mnangagwa? So is Zimbabwe doomed after 2030? https://www.mutapatimes.com/articles/2026-03-30-is-zanu-pf-so-useless-that-it-cant-continue-development-with.html Source: Is ZANU-PF so useless that it can’t continue development without Mnangagwa? So is Zimbabwe doomed after 2030? When praising a leader actually signals a weakness. Tendai Ruben Mbofana These are the questions that naturally arise when one examines the logic behind the Const Wed, 22 Apr 2026 19:06:19 +0000 https://www.mutapatimes.com/articles/2026-03-30-is-zanu-pf-so-useless-that-it-cant-continue-development-with.html Politics Zimbabwe Situation <media:content url="summary: "Source: Is ZANU-PF so useless that it can’t continue development without Mnangagwa? So is Zimbabwe doomed after 2030? When praising a leader actually signals a weakness. Tendai Ruben Mbofana These are the questions that naturally arise when one examines the logic behind the Const" medium="image"/>

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Zimbabwe considers social media ban for under-18s
item.description: The Zimbabwean government says it is drafting new legislation that could restrict children under the age of 18 from accessing social media platforms as part of efforts to improve online safety.The pro...
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News
